verdict_reasoning: BAX is a cytosolic/mitochondrial-outer-membrane pro-apoptotic BCL2 family effector. During apoptosis, BAX oligomerizes and permeabilizes the MOM, and externalization/exposure on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane of apoptotic cells has been documented (anti-BAX antibodies detect surface BAX on dying cells). This is a classic cell-death-induced ecto-display scenario, not baseline surface residency. No TM anchor at baseline, no GPI, no stable TM-partner tethering, no documented tissue-restricted surface lineage, not a lysosomal TM cargo, no baseline PM minority pool — but ICD/apoptosis-linked surface exposure qualifies as contextual.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of evidence for genuine outer-leaflet BAX display on apoptotic cells (vs. permeabilized-membrane access) is debated.
